lessons.title: Introduction & Overview
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"Introduction to DevOps DevOps is a culture and set of practices that aim to unify software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) to enhance collaboration, automate processes, and deliver software faster and more reliably. It breaks down silos between teams, fostering continuous integration, delivery, and feedback. By embracing automation, infrastructure as code, and continuous monitoring, DevOps streamlines workflows, reduces errors, and accelerates deployment cycles.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Introduction & Overview\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"The roots trace back to the early 2000s, when software development practices were evolving with Agile methodologies, focusing on faster, iterative delivery. However, deployments remained slow and error-prone due to disconnected workflows between developers and IT operations.","practiceTask":"Review Introduction & Overview and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

lessons.title: Introduction & Overview
